channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Nestlé CWAR Nesternship 2026: Fully Funded Internship Opportunity for African Students and Graduates
item.dc:creator: Susan Kamene
item.description:
If you are an ambitious student or recent graduate in Africa seeking a transformative career opportunity with a global brand, the Nestlé CWAR Nesternship 2026 is one of the most powerful internship programs currently open. Designed to bridge the gap between academic knowledge and real-world professional experience, this internship offers hands-on exposure, mentorship, and a […]

The post Nestlé CWAR Nesternship 2026: Fully Funded Internship Opportunity for African Students and Graduates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
If you are an ambitious student or recent graduate in Africa seeking a transformative career opportunity with a global brand, the Nestlé CWAR Nesternship 2026 is one of the most powerful internship programs currently open. Designed to bridge the gap between academic knowledge and real-world professional experience, this internship offers hands-on exposure, mentorship, and a potential pathway into full-time employment with Nestlé.

What is the Nestlé CWAR Nesternship Program?

The Nesternship is a flagship internship initiative by Nestlé designed to empower young African talent with practical work experience in a global corporate environment. It is part of Nestlé’s broader commitment to youth development under its “Needs YOUth” initiative.

Through this program, selected participants gain:

  • Hands-on experience working on real-life business projects
  • Direct mentorship from experienced professionals
  • Exposure to global business operations
  • Practical skills in key functional areas

The internship can be virtual or hybrid, allowing participants to work remotely or physically depending on location and company needs.

Why You Should Apply for the Nesternship

This is not just another internship—it is a career-launching platform. Here’s what makes it stand out:

1. Real-World Work Experience – Participants work on actual business projects across departments such as:

  • Finance and Accounting
  • Marketing and Communications
  • Sales
  • Engineering
  • Information Technology

2. Professional Mentorship – Interns are guided by industry experts who help shape their career paths and develop workplace-ready skills.

3. Paid Internship Opportunity – The program offers financial benefits including stipends and other rewards depending on the region.

4. Career Advancement Potential – Outstanding interns may gain opportunities for full-time roles or further engagement with Nestlé.

5. Flexible Work Structure – Participants can work remotely with just a computer and internet connection, making it accessible across Africa.

Who Can Apply? (Eligible Candidates)

The Nestlé CWAR Nesternship targets young Africans who are at the early stages of their careers.

Eligible Categories:

  • Undergraduate students
  • Recent graduates
  • Entry-level job seekers

Eligible Countries/Regions:

  • Countries within Central and West Africa, including:
    • Nigeria
    • Ghana
    • Côte d’Ivoire
    • Senegal
    • Burkina Faso
    • Other African countries within the CWAR region

Eligibility Requirements

To qualify for the Nesternship, applicants must meet the following criteria:

  • Be a full-time undergraduate student or recent graduate
  • Have a strong academic background (typically a minimum CGPA around 3.2 or equivalent)
  • Possess excellent communication and interpersonal skills
  • Demonstrate leadership potential and participation in extracurricular activities
  • Have access to a computer and reliable internet (for virtual roles)
  • Successfully complete the Talent Games assessment, a gamified evaluation process

Duration of the Internship

  • The program duration typically ranges from 1 month to 6 months, depending on availability and project requirements.

Benefits of the Nestlé CWAR Nesternship

Participants gain both tangible and intangible rewards, including:

Career Benefits:

  • Practical industry experience
  • Skill development in real business environments
  • Enhanced employability

Financial & Material Benefits:

  • Monthly stipend (varies by country)
  • Training and development resources
  • Internship completion certificate

Networking Opportunities:

  • Access to a global network of professionals
  • Collaboration with diverse teams

Application Process (Step-by-Step Guide)

Applying for the Nesternship involves a structured and competitive process:

Step 1: Online Registration

Applicants must sign up on the official Nestlé recruitment or Talent Games platform.

Step 2: Gamified Assessment

Candidates complete an interactive evaluation involving:

  • Case studies
  • Problem-solving tasks
  • Behavioral assessments

Step 3: Screening & Selection

Top-performing candidates are shortlisted and matched with suitable project teams.

Apply here

Key Skills You Will Develop

By participating in the Nesternship, you will gain:

  • Analytical and problem-solving skills
  • Communication and teamwork abilities
  • Leadership and project management experience
  • Industry-specific technical knowledge

Why This Opportunity Matters for African Youth

The Nestlé CWAR Nesternship addresses one of the biggest challenges facing young Africans today—the lack of practical work experience after graduation. By combining training, mentorship, and real-world exposure, the program helps bridge the gap between education and employment.

It is especially valuable in a competitive job market where employers prioritize experience alongside academic qualifications.

Deadline Information

  •  Not explicitly stated
  • However, applications are typically open for a short window (about 2 weeks in some cycles), making early application critical.

The Nestlé CWAR Nesternship 2026 is a highly competitive and career-defining internship opportunity for African students and graduates. With its strong focus on skill development, mentorship, and real-world experience, it provides a solid foundation for long-term professional success.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post Nestlé CWAR Nesternship 2026: Fully Funded Internship Opportunity for African Students and Graduates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Sawaba Fellowship 2026: Funded Opportunity for African Thinkers and Creatives
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
The Sawaba Fellowship offers a unique opportunity for African-based thinkers, artists, and organisers to receive funding, mentorship, and space to explore decolonial feminist ideas. Hosted by Liberation Alliance Africa, this three-month fellowship supports individuals committed to challenging colonial narratives and producing transformative knowledge rooted in African histories, memory, and lived realities. With a strong focus […]

The post Sawaba Fellowship 2026: Funded Opportunity for African Thinkers and Creatives appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Sawaba Fellowship offers a unique opportunity for African-based thinkers, artists, and organisers to receive funding, mentorship, and space to explore decolonial feminist ideas. Hosted by Liberation Alliance Africa, this three-month fellowship supports individuals committed to challenging colonial narratives and producing transformative knowledge rooted in African histories, memory, and lived realities. With a strong focus on interdisciplinary and practice-based work, the programme is designed for those seeking to deepen their impact while engaging in critical consciousness and collective reflection.

About the Program

The Sawaba Fellowship is an initiative of the Sawaba Institute of Critical Consciousness Raising, hosted by Liberation Alliance Africa. It brings together a small cohort of fellows from across the African continent to engage in research, storytelling, and creative practice that advances decolonial feminist futures.

The 2026 theme, “Not Pioneers of an Empty Land,” challenges dominant colonial narratives that erase indigenous histories, knowledge systems, and governance structures. Fellows are invited to interrogate and dismantle the myth that African societies lacked complexity prior to colonialism, instead centering ancestral memory, cultural continuity, and collective knowledge.

The fellowship begins with a fully funded in-person convening in May 2026, followed by a three-month period of project development and collaboration. Fellows are expected to produce original work aligned with the theme, which may take various forms including essays, research papers, artistic productions, exhibitions, or public engagements.

Why This Opportunity Matters

In a global landscape where colonial narratives continue to shape knowledge production and policy, opportunities like the Sawaba Fellowship are critical. They provide space for African voices to redefine frameworks of knowledge, identity, and justice on their own terms.

This fellowship goes beyond traditional academic or professional development programmes by prioritising lived experience, collective memory, and indigenous knowledge systems. It recognises that meaningful transformation requires not only new ideas but also a re-engagement with historical truths and suppressed narratives.

By supporting interdisciplinary work and community-rooted approaches, the programme contributes to broader efforts toward epistemic justice and sustainable, inclusive futures.

What Participants Will Gain

Participants in the Sawaba Fellowship will gain both tangible and intangible benefits that support their personal and professional growth.

Fellows receive a monthly stipend of USD 500, enabling them to focus on their projects without financial strain. The programme also includes a fully funded convening, offering a rare opportunity to connect in person with like-minded thinkers, artists, and organisers from across the continent.

Beyond financial support, fellows benefit from a collaborative learning environment that encourages critical dialogue, mentorship, and peer exchange. This creates a strong foundation for intellectual growth and long-term partnerships.

Participants also gain visibility for their work, as the fellowship supports the development of outputs that contribute to public discourse, whether through publications, artistic work, or community engagement. Ultimately, the experience equips fellows with the tools, networks, and confidence to continue advancing decolonial and feminist work beyond the programme.

Who Can Apply

The fellowship is open to individuals of African descent who are currently based on the African continent. Applicants may come from a wide range of disciplines and backgrounds, including academia, the arts, journalism, activism, and community organising.

Successful candidates are those who demonstrate a strong commitment to decolonial feminist politics, critical thinking, and social transformation. They should also be prepared to engage deeply with the fellowship theme and contribute meaningfully to collective learning spaces.

Application Process and Deadline

To apply, candidates must submit a complete application via email. This includes a cover letter, a project proposal outlining their intended work, a portfolio or CV showcasing relevant experience, and a draft budget.

Applications must be submitted by 24 April 2026. Due to the competitive nature of the fellowship, only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. Selected fellows must be available to attend the in-person convening in May 2026 and actively participate throughout the three-month programme.

Learn more about the program and requirements in the official description.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth

Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post Sawaba Fellowship 2026: Funded Opportunity for African Thinkers and Creatives appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: $1.1 Million Gates Foundation President, Global Policy and Advocacy Job in Seattle, USA | Apply Before May 1, 2026
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
The Gates Foundation has announced an exceptional executive career opportunity for experienced global leaders. Applications are now open for the role of President, Global Policy and Advocacy, based in Seattle, USA, with a salary range of $800,000 to $1,100,000 USD annually. This is one of the highest-paying nonprofit leadership jobs currently available worldwide, offering the […]

The post $1.1 Million Gates Foundation President, Global Policy and Advocacy Job in Seattle, USA | Apply Before May 1, 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Gates Foundation has announced an exceptional executive career opportunity for experienced global leaders. Applications are now open for the role of President, Global Policy and Advocacy, based in Seattle, USA, with a salary range of $800,000 to $1,100,000 USD annually.

This is one of the highest-paying nonprofit leadership jobs currently available worldwide, offering the chance to shape global policy, strengthen international partnerships, and help solve some of the world’s biggest challenges including poverty, disease, and inequality.

About the Gates Foundation

The Gates Foundation is the world’s largest nonprofit organization focused on fighting poverty, disease, and inequity. Through investments, partnerships, and advocacy, the foundation works to ensure every person has the opportunity to live a healthy and productive life.

The organization is also known for offering world-class employee benefits, including:

  • Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision coverage
  • No employee premiums
  • Generous paid time off
  • Paid family leave
  • Retirement contributions
  • Regional holidays
  • Employee communities and growth opportunities

About the Role

The President, Global Policy and Advocacy serves as the foundation’s top advisor on political, geopolitical, and government relations matters. This executive will work directly with the CEO, Governing Board, and senior leadership team to drive policy influence and build strategic partnerships around the world.

The selected candidate will lead the Global Policy and Advocacy division and oversee initiatives that advance the foundation’s priorities in global health, development, and U.S. domestic issues.

Key Responsibilities

1. Strategic Leadership

  • Advise executive leadership on global political developments
  • Guide decisions on risk management and policy direction
  • Support country-level strategies across multiple regions

2. Government Relations & Financing

  • Lead global government relations strategies
  • Build partnerships with donor governments and multilateral institutions
  • Support domestic resource mobilization and funding strategies

3. Global Partnerships

  • Build coalitions with governments, civil society, faith leaders, think tanks, and private sector stakeholders
  • Expand philanthropic giving in the U.S. and globally
  • Strengthen long-term support for health and development priorities

4. Public Representation

  • Represent the foundation at high-level global forums
  • Support strategic external engagements for senior leadership
  • Manage sensitive political relationships with professionalism and discretion

5. Organizational Leadership

  • Lead a globally distributed team
  • Align resources with long-term strategy
  • Ensure compliance, governance, and high performance across the division

Required Experience

Applicants should have:

  • Significant leadership experience in public policy, advocacy, diplomacy, or development
  • Deep understanding of global political systems
  • Experience managing large and complex international teams
  • Strong record of influencing public policy and institutional strategy
  • Exceptional communication and relationship-building skills

Preferred candidates may also have experience in:

  • Global health financing
  • Grantmaking
  • Strategic communications
  • Philanthropic partnerships
  • Investment portfolio management

Salary and Benefits

The salary for this executive role ranges from:

$800,000 – $1,100,000 USD per year

In addition to salary, employees receive an outstanding benefits package that supports health, family, retirement, and career growth.

Application Deadline

May 1, 2026

Late applications may not be considered, so interested candidates should apply as early as possible.

How to Apply

Applications are being managed through Russell Reynolds Associates.

Interested candidates should apply via the official platform using career code: 2604-021NA

APPLY HERE. 

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Discover more job opportunities, click here.

Why This Opportunity Matters

This role is more than a job—it is a chance to influence global policy, reduce poverty, improve health outcomes, and help millions of people worldwide. For qualified senior leaders seeking impact-driven executive careers, this is a rare and prestigious opportunity.

The post $1.1 Million Gates Foundation President, Global Policy and Advocacy Job in Seattle, USA | Apply Before May 1, 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Oxford Global Health Research Diploma: 30 Scholarships for African Professionals
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
The Oxford Global Health Research Diploma 2026 offers funded scholarships for African professionals to pursue a fully online postgraduate qualification designed to build expertise in global health research. Delivered by the University of Oxford, this part-time programme enables participants to gain advanced research skills while continuing to work in their current roles. About the Program […]

The post Oxford Global Health Research Diploma: 30 Scholarships for African Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Oxford Global Health Research Diploma 2026 offers funded scholarships for African professionals to pursue a fully online postgraduate qualification designed to build expertise in global health research. Delivered by the University of Oxford, this part-time programme enables participants to gain advanced research skills while continuing to work in their current roles.

About the Program

The Postgraduate Diploma in Global Health Research is a part-time, fully online programme delivered over 14 months. It is designed for professionals involved in global health research, including those working in research institutions, healthcare systems, policy environments, and organisations that fund or regulate research.

The course provides a comprehensive understanding of the entire research cycle—from developing research questions to translating findings into policy and practice. A key focus of the programme is addressing health challenges in low-resource settings, with strong emphasis on ethical considerations, regulatory frameworks, and context-specific solutions.

Participants will engage in practical, applied learning using real-world case studies. The programme encourages collaboration among a diverse cohort of students from different professional and geographic backgrounds, creating a rich learning environment that reflects global health realities.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Global health research continues to face significant inequities, particularly in who leads research and where it is conducted. Many professionals in low-resource settings face barriers to accessing high-quality postgraduate education, including financial constraints and the need to relocate.

This programme directly addresses these challenges by offering flexible, online learning combined with targeted scholarship support. By enabling professionals to study without leaving their jobs or communities, the programme helps build local research capacity and promotes more equitable global health systems.

The initiative also supports the development of leaders who can design and implement research that is relevant, ethical, and impactful in their local contexts. This contributes to stronger health systems and more effective responses to global health challenges.

What Participants Will Gain

Participants will gain advanced knowledge and practical skills in designing, conducting, and managing global health research. The programme equips students with the ability to develop high-quality, ethically sound studies and translate research findings into actionable policies and practices.

Students will also develop strong analytical, critical thinking, and problem-solving skills, which are essential for addressing complex health challenges. The course enhances communication and academic writing abilities, enabling participants to effectively present and publish research findings.

In addition, participants benefit from being part of a global network of professionals and researchers. This network provides opportunities for collaboration, knowledge exchange, and long-term career development.

For scholarship recipients, financial support significantly reduces the cost of study, making this high-quality education more accessible. The flexibility of the programme also allows participants to continue contributing to their organisations while advancing their qualifications.

Who Can Apply

The programme is open to professionals involved in global health research or related fields, including those working in research sites, healthcare organisations, regulatory bodies, and funding institutions.

Applicants must meet the academic requirements for postgraduate study and demonstrate a strong interest in global health research. The programme is particularly suited for individuals who want to strengthen their research skills and contribute to improving health outcomes in low-resource settings.

Eligible Areas

The Africa Oxford Initiative is offering 30 competitive scholarships specifically for African applicants. These scholarships are intended to support individuals taking their first step into postgraduate study.

While the programme itself is open to applicants globally, the funded scholarship opportunities highlighted here are targeted at candidates from Africa.

Application Process and Deadline

Shortlisted candidates may be required to provide additional information or participate in an interview as part of the scholarship selection process.

Applicants are encouraged to carefully review the eligibility criteria and prepare a strong application that demonstrates their academic background, professional experience, and motivation for pursuing the programme.

To apply, candidates must submit an application through the University of Oxford admissions portal for the Postgraduate Diploma in Global Health Research.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth

Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post Oxford Global Health Research Diploma: 30 Scholarships for African Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Falling Walls Lab Gauteng 2026 in South Africa: Win a Trip to Berlin Global Finale
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
Applications are now open for Falling Walls Lab Gauteng 2026, an exciting platform for emerging talents, innovators, and visionaries to showcase breakthrough ideas that can positively transform science and society. If you have a research concept, startup solution, or bold innovation, this is your chance to pitch it in just three minutes before a distinguished […]

The post Falling Walls Lab Gauteng 2026 in South Africa: Win a Trip to Berlin Global Finale appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for Falling Walls Lab Gauteng 2026, an exciting platform for emerging talents, innovators, and visionaries to showcase breakthrough ideas that can positively transform science and society. If you have a research concept, startup solution, or bold innovation, this is your chance to pitch it in just three minutes before a distinguished audience and expert jury.

Hosted in Gauteng, South Africa, this prestigious event is part of the global Falling Walls Lab network that identifies groundbreaking ideas from future leaders around the world.

What is Falling Walls Lab Gauteng?

Falling Walls Lab is an international pitch competition that gives young innovators the opportunity to present their ideas in a concise three-minute format. Participants are challenged to answer one key question:

Which wall will your research break?

Whether your innovation tackles challenges in health, education, sustainability, engineering, technology, or social development, this platform helps bring your ideas to the world stage.

Why You Should Apply

Selected participants at Falling Walls Lab Gauteng will gain access to exceptional opportunities, including:

  • Present your breakthrough idea to experts and innovators
  • Expand your professional and academic network
  • Gain visibility for your research or startup idea
  • Connect with global changemakers
  • Compete for a fully funded trip to Berlin
  • Chance to participate in the Falling Walls Lab Global Finale in Germany

Event Details

Event Name: Falling Walls Lab Gauteng 2026
Date: 5 August 2026
Format: Hybrid Event (In-person + Livestream)
Venue: WITS University (University of the Witwatersrand), Johannesburg, Gauteng, South Africa

Who Should Apply?

This opportunity is ideal for:

  • University students
  • Graduates
  • Researchers
  • Innovators
  • Early-career professionals
  • Startup founders
  • Young leaders with impactful ideas

Applicants from all disciplines are encouraged to apply.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should submit their applications online through the official portal below:

APPLY HERE. 

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Discover more competition opportunities, click here. 

Deadline

Application Deadline: 30 April 2026

Contact Information

For enquiries, contact:
daad@wits.ac.za
Elizabeth.Maseko@wits.ac.za

Final Thoughts

Falling Walls Lab Gauteng 2026 is more than a competition—it is a gateway to global recognition, collaboration, and impact. If you have an idea capable of breaking barriers and shaping the future, this is the perfect platform to share it. Apply now and take your innovation from Gauteng to Berlin.

The post Falling Walls Lab Gauteng 2026 in South Africa: Win a Trip to Berlin Global Finale appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Kofi Annan Accountability Hubs Europe 2026: Nominate a Young Leader for a Prestigious Governance Initiative
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
The Kofi Annan Accountability Hubs Europe 2026 initiative offers a unique opportunity for young leaders to strengthen democratic accountability, transparency, and youth participation across Europe through a fully supported leadership platform. This program is designed for emerging changemakers who are already working to improve governance systems and mobilise communities for more inclusive decision-making. About the […]

The post Kofi Annan Accountability Hubs Europe 2026: Nominate a Young Leader for a Prestigious Governance Initiative appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Kofi Annan Accountability Hubs Europe 2026 initiative offers a unique opportunity for young leaders to strengthen democratic accountability, transparency, and youth participation across Europe through a fully supported leadership platform. This program is designed for emerging changemakers who are already working to improve governance systems and mobilise communities for more inclusive decision-making.

About the Program

The Accountability Hubs Europe initiative is led by the Kofi Annan Foundation in partnership with the Westminster Foundation for Democracy (WFD). Building on the success of similar programs in Sub-Saharan Africa, this new European edition will bring together 10 outstanding young leaders from across the region.

The program focuses on strengthening democratic accountability by supporting youth leaders who are actively working in areas such as governance, civic engagement, and transparency. Selected participants will join a network of peers committed to advancing accountability systems and increasing meaningful youth participation in democratic processes.

Participants will engage in collaborative activities, knowledge exchange, and leadership development opportunities. The initiative is designed to connect individuals across sectors—including civil society, politics, media, business, and the arts—who are working toward more inclusive and accountable governance.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Across Europe, democratic systems are facing increasing challenges, including declining trust in institutions, reduced civic participation, and growing inequalities in representation. Young people play a critical role in addressing these challenges by bringing new perspectives, innovative approaches, and community-driven solutions.

The Accountability Hubs initiative recognizes that young leaders are essential to strengthening democratic systems. By investing in their leadership and connecting them across borders, the program aims to create a new generation of changemakers who can influence policy, foster transparency, and promote inclusive governance.

This opportunity is particularly valuable for those already engaged in advocacy or community work, as it provides a platform to scale their impact and connect with a broader European network.

What Participants Will Gain

Participants selected for the Accountability Hubs Europe program will benefit from a range of professional and leadership development opportunities. They will join a cohort of like-minded young leaders, enabling collaboration and peer learning across different countries and sectors.

The program offers access to international networks, including connections with leading organisations working in governance and democracy. This exposure can significantly enhance participants’ ability to influence policy and drive change within their communities.

Participants will also strengthen their leadership and advocacy skills through structured engagement, discussions, and collaborative initiatives. The experience supports both personal growth and professional development, helping young leaders refine their strategies for promoting accountability and transparency.

Additionally, being part of a prestigious initiative led by globally recognized organizations enhances credibility and visibility, which can open doors to future opportunities in leadership, policy, and international development.

Who Can Apply

This opportunity is open to young leaders aged 18 to 35 who are actively engaged in promoting accountability, youth participation, or inclusive governance. Candidates should demonstrate a strong track record of leadership and community engagement.

Applicants must be able to mobilise and connect diverse youth communities and show a commitment to strengthening democratic systems. The program seeks individuals from a variety of sectors, including civil society, politics, business, media, and the arts.

Nominations must be submitted by an organisation, meaning individuals cannot self-nominate. Organizations are encouraged to nominate candidates who have demonstrated impact and leadership potential.

Eligible Areas

The program is open to young leaders from across Europe. Applicants must be based in or actively working within the European region.

Application Process and Deadline

Candidates must be nominated by an organisation through the official application process. The nomination should include relevant details about the candidate’s work, leadership experience, and impact.

The deadline for nominations is April 27, 2026 (midnight CEST). Interested organisations should ensure that nominations are submitted before the deadline, as late submissions will not be considered.

Due to the competitive nature of the program, nominators are encouraged to highlight the candidate’s achievements, leadership potential, and commitment to democratic accountability.

Learn more on the Kofi Annan Foundation website.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth

Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post Kofi Annan Accountability Hubs Europe 2026: Nominate a Young Leader for a Prestigious Governance Initiative appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Anthropic Fellows Program 2026: Paid AI Research Fellowship with Mentorship, Funding & Career Opportunities
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
Launch Your AI Career with the Anthropic Fellows Program If you are graduating from university or starting your professional journey in tech, AI, research, or engineering, the Anthropic Fellows Program 2026 could be one of the most valuable opportunities available this year. Designed for emerging technical talent, this highly competitive fellowship offers direct mentorship from […]

The post Anthropic Fellows Program 2026: Paid AI Research Fellowship with Mentorship, Funding & Career Opportunities appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Launch Your AI Career with the Anthropic Fellows Program

If you are graduating from university or starting your professional journey in tech, AI, research, or engineering, the Anthropic Fellows Program 2026 could be one of the most valuable opportunities available this year.

Designed for emerging technical talent, this highly competitive fellowship offers direct mentorship from world-class AI researchers, generous weekly funding, access to cutting-edge research infrastructure, and the opportunity to work on impactful real-world projects in AI safety, machine learning, reinforcement learning, security, and societal impact.

Even more exciting, strong performance during the fellowship may lead to future full-time opportunities with Anthropic or help launch your career at other top AI organizations.

What Is the Anthropic Fellows Program?

The Anthropic Fellows Program is a 4-month, full-time paid fellowship created to develop the next generation of AI researchers and engineers.

Participants work on empirical research projects aligned with Anthropic’s priorities and receive mentorship while building public outputs such as research papers, technical tools, or open-source contributions.

According to previous cohorts, over 80% of fellows successfully produced papers, and 25% to 50% received full-time job offers.

Why This Fellowship Is Worth Applying For

This program is especially powerful for graduates and early-career applicants because it values potential, not just previous experience.

Anthropic explicitly states that promising technical talent can apply regardless of prior research background.

Key Benefits of the Anthropic Fellowship

Paid Weekly Stipend

Fellows receive:

  • $3,850 USD per week
  • £2,310 GBP per week
  • $4,300 CAD per week

For 40 hours per week over 4 months.

Mentorship from Top AI Researchers

You will receive direct guidance from leading experts in AI safety, security, interpretability, reinforcement learning, and economics.

Research Funding

Participants receive:

  • Approximately $15,000/month in compute resources
  • Additional funding for research-related expenses

Global Work Flexibility

Available in:

  • London, UK
  • Berkeley / San Francisco Bay Area, California
  • Ontario, Canada
  • Remote within eligible countries

Career Growth Opportunities

Past fellows have gone on to:

  • Receive full-time offers from Anthropic
  • Publish papers
  • Build networks in the global AI research ecosystem
  • Transition into advanced technical careers

Fellowship Workstreams Available

Applicants may be considered across multiple tracks.

1. AI Safety Fellows

Work on alignment, scalable oversight, adversarial robustness, interpretability, AI welfare, and safety evaluations.

2. AI Security Fellows

Focus on red teaming, model vulnerabilities, offensive security, and safeguarding advanced AI systems.

3. ML Systems & Performance Fellows

Build infrastructure, optimize systems, support accelerator workloads, and improve large-scale ML operations.

4. Reinforcement Learning Fellows

Research training systems, RL environments, generalization, and model improvement.

5. Economics & Societal Impacts Fellows

Study AI’s effect on labor markets, society, well-being, public policy, and human-AI collaboration.

Who Should Apply?

This fellowship is ideal for applicants who:

  • Want to transition into AI research
  • Have strong technical foundations
  • Enjoy solving difficult real-world problems
  • Can code in Python
  • Communicate clearly and collaborate well
  • Care about safe and beneficial AI development
  • Want mentorship and career acceleration

You do not need to meet every listed qualification to apply.

Important Eligibility Requirements

To participate, applicants must:

  • Have legal work authorization in the US, UK, or Canada
  • Be located in one of those countries during the fellowship
  • Commit full-time for 4 months
  • Be fluent in Python programming

Anthropic currently does not sponsor visas for fellows.

Important Deadline

Apply by April 26, 2026

The next fellowship cohort begins on July 20, 2026.

Applications after the deadline may still be considered for future rolling cohorts.

Why Graduates Should Not Miss This Opportunity

Many graduates struggle to gain experience because entry-level jobs often require prior experience. This program helps solve that problem by giving you:

  • Real research experience
  • Elite mentorship
  • Strong resume credentials
  • Paid technical work
  • Industry connections
  • Potential job pathways

For anyone serious about AI, machine learning, research, or emerging technology, this is a standout opportunity.

How to Apply

Apply through Anthropic’s official recruiting partner portal.

APPLY HERE. 

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Discover more fellowship opportunities, click here. 

Final Thoughts

The Anthropic Fellows Program 2026 is more than a fellowship—it is a launchpad for future researchers, engineers, and innovators.

If you are early in your career and want to work on meaningful AI problems while being paid, mentored, and connected to top talent, this opportunity deserves your immediate attention.

Apply before the deadline and take the next step in your future.

The post Anthropic Fellows Program 2026: Paid AI Research Fellowship with Mentorship, Funding & Career Opportunities appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: BA/Leverhulme Small Research Grants 2025–2026: Fully Funded Research Opportunity Worth Up to £10,000 for Scholars in Humanities and Social Sciences
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The British Academy, in partnership with the Leverhulme Trust, offers one of the most prestigious and competitive funding opportunities for researchers worldwide—the BA/Leverhulme Small Research Grants. This funding programme is designed to support innovative, high-quality research projects in the humanities and social sciences, providing scholars with financial backing to advance their academic work. What Is […]

The post BA/Leverhulme Small Research Grants 2025–2026: Fully Funded Research Opportunity Worth Up to £10,000 for Scholars in Humanities and Social Sciences appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The British Academy, in partnership with the Leverhulme Trust, offers one of the most prestigious and competitive funding opportunities for researchers worldwide—the BA/Leverhulme Small Research Grants. This funding programme is designed to support innovative, high-quality research projects in the humanities and social sciences, providing scholars with financial backing to advance their academic work.

What Is the BA/Leverhulme Small Research Grant?

The BA/Leverhulme Small Research Grants scheme is a flagship funding programme aimed at supporting primary research projects in the humanities and social sciences. It is one of the most popular and widely recognized funding schemes administered by the British Academy.

These grants are specifically designed to:

  • Enable innovative research projects
  • Support early-stage project development
  • Fund collaborations and academic networking
  • Facilitate data collection, fieldwork, and dissemination

Each grant provides up to £10,000, making it ideal for small-scale yet high-impact research initiatives.

Funding Coverage and What the Grant Supports

The BA/Leverhulme Small Research Grants are highly flexible and can be used for a wide range of research-related expenses, including:

  • Travel and subsistence for research activities
  • Access to archives, libraries, and databases
  • Research assistance and materials
  • Workshops, conferences, and collaboration meetings
  • Project planning and development costs

However, it is important to note that the grant does not cover salaries or major equipment such as computers.

Who Can Apply? (Eligibility Criteria)

Understanding eligibility is crucial for applicants. This programme is primarily targeted at postdoctoral-level researchers.

Eligible Applicants

  • Postdoctoral researchers or equivalent scholars
  • Academics affiliated with recognized institutions
  • Independent researchers with a proven track record

Residency Requirement

  • The Principal Applicant must be ordinarily resident in the United Kingdom

International Applicants

  • Researchers from any country or continent can participate as co-applicants
  • International collaboration is encouraged
  • However, the lead applicant must be UK-based

Target Audience: Who Should Apply?

This opportunity is ideal for:

  • Early-career and mid-career researchers
  • Academics in universities and research institutions
  • Independent scholars in humanities and social sciences
  • Research teams seeking seed funding for new ideas

It is particularly valuable for researchers looking to:

  • Test new research ideas
  • Build collaborative networks
  • Generate preliminary data for larger grants

Eligible Fields of Study

The grant supports research across all disciplines within:

  • Humanities (e.g., history, literature, philosophy)
  • Social Sciences (e.g., sociology, economics, political science)

The British Academy welcomes diverse methodologies and interdisciplinary approaches, provided the research demonstrates strong academic merit.

Apply here

Duration and Project Timeline

  • Minimum Duration: 1 month
  • Maximum Duration: 24 months

Projects must be clearly defined with specific objectives and measurable outcomes.

Key Features and Benefits

1. Prestigious Recognition

Being funded by the British Academy significantly enhances your academic profile.

2. Flexible Funding

You can tailor the grant to suit your research needs, from travel to workshops.

3. International Collaboration

While UK-based leadership is required, the scheme actively supports global partnerships.

4. High Success Visibility

The programme is one of the largest funding streams of the British Academy, with a strong track record of supporting scholars.

What Makes a Strong Application?

To increase your chances of success, your proposal should:

  • Clearly define a focused research question
  • Demonstrate academic significance and originality
  • Present a realistic methodology and timeline
  • Justify the budget effectively

Applications are assessed on quality, and in some cases, a random selection process is used among high-quality proposals to ensure fairness.

Important Restrictions and Conditions

  • Minimum funding request: £500
  • Maximum funding: £10,000
  • Cannot fund PhD thesis work directly
  • Cannot cover salaries or teaching replacement

Projects must produce clear academic outputs, such as publications, reports, or scholarly contributions.

Why This Grant Matters for Researchers Worldwide

Even though the scheme requires a UK-based lead applicant, it remains highly relevant globally because:

  • It promotes international research collaboration
  • It allows co-investigators from any country
  • It supports globally relevant research topics

This makes it a strategic opportunity for researchers in Africa, Asia, Europe, and the Americas to collaborate with UK-based scholars.

Deadline

27th May 2026

The BA/Leverhulme Small Research Grants stand out as one of the most accessible and impactful funding opportunities for scholars in the humanities and social sciences. With up to £10,000 in funding, flexible usage, and support for international collaboration, this grant provides a powerful platform for advancing research careers.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post BA/Leverhulme Small Research Grants 2025–2026: Fully Funded Research Opportunity Worth Up to £10,000 for Scholars in Humanities and Social Sciences appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: New Zealand UN Junior Adviser Programme 2027: Paid Internship Opportunity at the United Nations in New York
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The New Zealand Permanent Mission to the United Nations is offering an exciting and highly competitive Junior Adviser Programme for 2027. This prestigious opportunity provides early-career professionals and students with direct exposure to global diplomacy, international policymaking, and the inner workings of the United Nations. Designed as a short-term internship-style development programme, this role allows […]

The post New Zealand UN Junior Adviser Programme 2027: Paid Internship Opportunity at the United Nations in New York appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The New Zealand Permanent Mission to the United Nations is offering an exciting and highly competitive Junior Adviser Programme for 2027. This prestigious opportunity provides early-career professionals and students with direct exposure to global diplomacy, international policymaking, and the inner workings of the United Nations.

Designed as a short-term internship-style development programme, this role allows selected candidates to actively contribute to New Zealand’s foreign policy priorities while gaining hands-on experience in multilateral diplomacy at the highest level.

Overview of the Junior Adviser Programme

The Junior Adviser Programme places selected individuals at the New Zealand Permanent Mission to the United Nations in New York, where they will support diplomatic operations and policy implementation.

This opportunity is structured to:

  • Provide practical experience in international relations and diplomacy
  • Offer direct exposure to United Nations processes and negotiations
  • Enable participants to contribute to real-world policy discussions
  • Develop professional skills in research, communication, and reporting

According to the official call, this programme is intended to help participants “bring New Zealand’s foreign policy to life on the international community’s largest stage.”

Programme Duration and Timeline

There are six Junior Adviser positions available, each lasting between five to six months.

Available Cohorts:

  1. February 1, 2027 – July 31, 2027 (2 positions)
  2. June 1, 2027 – November 30, 2027
  3. August 1, 2027 – December 31, 2027
  4. August 1, 2027 – January 30, 2028 (2 positions)

Applicants must clearly indicate their availability in their application.

About the New Zealand Permanent Mission to the United Nations

The New Zealand Permanent Mission represents the New Zealand Government in multilateral diplomacy and international development at the United Nations.

Its core responsibilities include:

  • Building diplomatic relationships with UN Member States
  • Engaging with UN agencies and international bodies
  • Advancing New Zealand’s foreign policy priorities
  • Supporting international cooperation and development

The Mission emphasizes values such as:

  • Impact – delivering meaningful outcomes
  • Kotahitanga – strength through diversity
  • Courage – doing the right thing
  • Manaakitanga – respect and care for others

Key Responsibilities of Junior Advisers

Selected candidates will play an active role in supporting diplomatic work and policy implementation.

Core Tasks Include:

  1. Monitoring and Reporting
    • Attend UN meetings and events
    • Report on key discussions and outcomes
  2. Policy Research
    • Track global issues and UN processes
    • Conduct research aligned with foreign policy priorities
  3. Documentation and Communication
    • Prepare briefing notes and reports
    • Draft written summaries and updates
  4. Event and Administrative Support
    • Assist with official visits and events
    • Provide logistical and administrative support
  5. Digital Communication
    • Develop and manage social media content

These tasks provide a well-rounded experience in both policy and operational aspects of diplomacy.

Eligibility Criteria

The programme is open to individuals at different early career stages.

Eligible Applicants Include:

  • Recent graduates
  • Early-career professionals
  • Postgraduate students nearing completion

Citizenship Requirements:

  • Priority is given to New Zealand citizens
  • Non-New Zealand citizens may apply if they have valid authorization to work in the United States

Required Skills and Competencies

Applicants must demonstrate a strong combination of academic knowledge and professional skills.

Essential Skills:

  • Excellent written and verbal communication
  • Strong analytical and research abilities
  • Effective time management and organization
  • Ability to work independently and in teams
  • High level of integrity and professionalism

Desired Attributes:

  • Flexibility and adaptability
  • Attention to detail
  • Problem-solving ability
  • Strong interpersonal skills

Preferred Academic Backgrounds

While applicants from diverse disciplines are encouraged, the following fields are particularly relevant:

  • International Relations
  • Political Science
  • International Law
  • Economics
  • Human Rights
  • Sustainable Development
  • Public Health
  • Gender Studies
  • Communications
  • Security and Disarmament Studies

Compensation and Financial Information

The programme offers financial support, but applicants should note certain responsibilities.

What is Provided:

  • Monthly stipend of USD $3,000

What is Not Covered:

  • Flights to and from New York
  • Accommodation
  • Personal expenses

Applicants must also:

  • Arrange their own health insurance
  • Understand and comply with tax obligations

Visa Requirements

  • New Zealand citizens may receive visa support
  • Non-citizens must already have valid work authorization in the United States

Additional Requirements

Selected candidates will be required to:

  • Sign a confidentiality and participation agreement
  • Comply with health and safety protocols
  • Provide proof of insurance

Application Process

Applicants must follow specific instructions to be considered.

Required Documents:

  • Cover letter outlining interest and suitability
  • CV/Resume

Submission Guidelines:

  • Combine documents into one PDF
  • Email to: nzpmun@mfat.govt.nz
  • Subject line: “Application – Junior Adviser”

Additional Notes:

  • Include availability dates
  • State visa/citizenship status
  • Provide referees if shortlisted

Application Deadline

All applications must be submitted by:

May 1, 2026 (5:00 PM EST)

Late or incomplete applications will not be considered.

Why You Should Apply

This is a rare opportunity to gain first-hand experience in global diplomacy.

Key Benefits:

  • Work at the United Nations in New York
  • Gain practical experience in international policy
  • Build a global professional network
  • Enhance research, communication, and leadership skills
  • Contribute to real-world diplomatic processes

Click HERE to learn more about the New Zealand UN Junior Adviser Programme 2027.

Discover more global Internship opportunities on OFY.

The post New Zealand UN Junior Adviser Programme 2027: Paid Internship Opportunity at the United Nations in New York appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Fulbright African Research Scholar Program (ARSP) 2027–2028: Fully Funded Research Grants in the USA
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
The Fulbright Program African Research Scholar Program (ARSP) is a prestigious fully funded opportunity designed for African scholars, university faculty, and research professionals to conduct advanced research and academic development in the United States. The program supports scholars across all disciplines and aims to strengthen academic institutions, research capacity, and knowledge exchange between Africa and […]

The post Fulbright African Research Scholar Program (ARSP) 2027–2028: Fully Funded Research Grants in the USA appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Fulbright Program African Research Scholar Program (ARSP) is a prestigious fully funded opportunity designed for African scholars, university faculty, and research professionals to conduct advanced research and academic development in the United States.

The program supports scholars across all disciplines and aims to strengthen academic institutions, research capacity, and knowledge exchange between Africa and the United States.

Key Details of the Fulbright ARSP

  • Program: Fulbright African Research Scholar Program (ARSP)
  • Location: United States
  • Funding: Fully funded
  • Eligible Applicants: African university faculty, researchers, and administrators
  • Duration: 3 to 9 months (depending on grant type)
  • Study Level: Research and academic development (non-degree)

Types of Fulbright ARSP Grants

The program offers two main categories of grants:

Research Grants

  • Duration: 3 to 9 months
  • For: University faculty and research institute professionals
  • Purpose: Conduct independent research at U.S. academic or research institutions
  • Requirement: Applicants must hold a doctorate or equivalent terminal degree
  • Preference:
    • At least three years of university teaching experience
    • Strong academic and research track record

Program and Curriculum Development Grants

  • Duration: 3 to 5 months
  • For: University faculty or administrators
  • Purpose: Develop new courses, curricula, or academic programs
  • Requirement: Minimum of a master’s degree or equivalent
  • Ideal for:
    • Early- to mid-career academics
    • Those with limited exposure to recent research or teaching innovations

Fulbright ARSP Benefits

The Fulbright ARSP provides comprehensive funding to support scholars during their stay in the United States. Benefits typically include:

  • International travel (round-trip airfare)
  • Monthly stipend for living expenses
  • Health insurance coverage
  • Institutional affiliation support

Additional funding for laboratory fees may be considered on a limited, case-by-case basis where applicable.

Eligibility Criteria

To apply for the Fulbright ARSP, applicants must meet the following general requirements:

  • Be a citizen or permanent resident of an eligible African country
  • Hold the required academic qualifications:
    • Doctorate for research grants
    • Master’s degree for program and curriculum development grants
  • Demonstrate strong academic or professional experience
  • Have sufficient proficiency in English
  • Be in good health and able to complete the program

Additional Considerations

  • Preference is given to applicants who:
    • Have not previously received a Fulbright Scholar grant
    • Have limited or no prior experience in the United States
  • Ineligible applications include:
    • Doctoral dissertation research
    • Postdoctoral research immediately after completing a PhD
    • General professional travel

Important Program Restrictions

  • Clinical medical research involving patient contact is not permitted
  • Laboratory fees are not automatically covered and require additional funding
  • Applicants must present original work and adhere strictly to plagiarism policies

Application Process

The application process for the Fulbright ARSP is conducted entirely online.

Steps to Apply

  • Create an account on the official application portal
  • Complete the online application form
  • Select your preferred grant category (Research or Program Development)
  • Upload all required documents
  • Submit three letters of reference
  • Provide a research proposal or academic development plan

Supporting Documents

  • Academic transcripts and certificates
  • Curriculum Vitae (CV)
  • Personal statement or research proposal
  • Letters of recommendation

Letters of invitation from U.S. institutions are encouraged but not mandatory.

Application Deadline

  • Deadline: Varies by country (typically mid-year)

Applicants should confirm exact deadlines and procedures through their local Fulbright Commission, Foundation, or U.S. Embassy.

Why Apply for the Fulbright ARSP

The Fulbright ARSP is more than a research grant—it is a platform for academic leadership and global collaboration.

Key benefits include:

  • Access to leading U.S. universities and research institutions
  • Opportunity to collaborate with international scholars
  • Strengthening of teaching, research, and institutional capacity
  • Development of innovative academic programs
  • Contribution to national and regional development in Africa

Pro Tip for Applicants

A strong application should clearly demonstrate:

  • The relevance and impact of your research or academic project
  • Alignment with your professional responsibilities
  • Practical outcomes for your home institution
  • Clear plans for knowledge transfer after the program

Well-structured proposals with measurable impact stand out in the selection process.

How to Apply

Applicants must submit their applications through the official Fulbright online portal and follow country-specific guidelines provided by local Fulbright offices.

 Apply here: https://apply.iie.org/fvsp2027  

Conclusion

The Fulbright Program African Research Scholar Program (ARSP) offers a unique opportunity for African academics and professionals to advance their research, strengthen institutional capacity, and build global partnerships.

For scholars committed to academic excellence and impact, ARSP provides a pathway to meaningful research collaboration and long-term development outcomes.

For more fully funded scholarships, research grants, and global opportunities, visit: https://opportunitiesforyouth.org/?s=research

For more opportunities for Africans, visit https://opportunitiesforyouth.org/?s=Africa

The post Fulbright African Research Scholar Program (ARSP) 2027–2028: Fully Funded Research Grants in the USA appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
